authoritative_sources.title: 2025 National Guidelines for a Behavioral Health Coordinated System of Crisis Care
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's national crisis-care guidance is the primary federal operating baseline for crisis-system design, care transitions, warm handoffs, follow-up expectations, and accountable coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Crisis dependency intake and continuity-of-care routing
used_for: defining the crisis continuum and handoff stages
failure modes when crisis systems are fragmented

authoritative_sources.title: Model Definitions for Behavioral Health Emergency, Crisis, and Crisis-Related Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA model definitions are the authoritative public terminology baseline for warm handoff, crisis facility, crisis response, follow-up, and mobile crisis service definitions used in this lane.
used_for: separating crisis coordination from adjacent execution

authoritative_sources.title: State Health Official Letter SHO# 21-008 RE: Medicaid Guidance on the Scope of and Payments for Qualifying Community-Based Mobile Crisis Intervention Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS mobile-crisis guidance is the authoritative federal payer and provider operating reference for qualifying mobile crisis workflows, warm handoffs, community follow-up, and coordination expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Mobile crisis routing and receiving-program transition
used_for: identifying where payer or admission authority sits elsewhere

authoritative_sources.title: CCBHC Certification Criteria: Implementation Deadlines
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's CCBHC criteria page is an authoritative public provider-program source for 24/7 crisis access, care coordination, and health-information expectations in community behavioral-health operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Community behavioral-health ownership and systems-of-record expectations
used_for: distinguishing crisis coordination from broader care management

authoritative_sources.title: 988 Lifeline Minimum Standards for Crisis Centers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The 988 Lifeline minimum standards are the recognized public operating rules for network crisis centers, covering active engagement, warm transfers, referrals, follow-up obligations, and imminent-risk handling.
used_for: when imminent-risk or supervisor involvement blocks generic routing

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Sharing Information Related to Mental Health
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR's mental-health sharing guidance is the authoritative public privacy reference for when behavioral-health information may be shared during crisis coordination and when permission or professional judgment limits apply.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy-aware evidence routing and disclosure control

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 2 - Confidentiality of Substance Use Disorder Patient Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 42 CFR Part 2 is the primary U.S. confidentiality rule for SUD treatment records and imposes stricter disclosure limits that materially change some behavioral-health crisis handoffs.

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the crisis episode, prior owner, candidate next owner, jurisdiction, required records, and current blocker state before any handoff or closure claim.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team routing and handoff packaging
workflow_stages.description: Normalize crisis terminology, confirm owner transitions, assemble the minimum artifact bundle, and prepare handoff outputs without implying clinical or legal authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, safety, and authority-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Preserve safety posture, disclosure controls, and ownership when privacy constraints, imminent-risk signals, detention rules, or payer decisions prevent confident routing.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and follow-up ownership
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when the destination owner, receiving-program acceptance, follow-up owner, and residual blocker state are all explicit; otherwise keep the case open or escalate.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: 988 or local crisis contact episode records
mobile crisis referral and dispatch records
safety plan status and crisis assessment summaries owned by clinical staff
consent posture and privacy or Part 2 disclosure documentation
follow-up outreach commitments and owner acknowledgements
handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing notes, and closure confirmations
systems_of_record: mobile crisis dispatch or CAD system

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for crisis services coordination
organize authoritative records, required artifacts, next owners, and blocker states for crisis handoffs
summarize crisis-continuum dependencies and closure conditions without implying final clinical, legal, or payer authority
escalate: clinical risk determination, involuntary hold, emergency detention, or treatment decisions requiring licensed or authorized owners
privacy, HIPAA, or 42 CFR Part 2 disclosure questions that materially change what can be shared
payer authorization, admission criteria, or financial approval questions outside coordination authority
conflicting public, jurisdictional, or tenant policy that materially changes routing, disclosure, or follow-up handling
refuse: disclose behavioral-health or SUD records without documented authority
close a crisis handoff without receiving-program acceptance and explicit follow-up ownership
treat crisis coordination like generic scheduling or project management when crisis artifacts and safety posture are required
claim authority to direct transport, detention, admission, or clinical disposition

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative crisis episode, location, contactability, consent, or receiving-program evidence.
Multiple teams appear to own the same crisis step and the conflict is collapsed into a generic handoff instead of an explicit escalation.
The lane absorbs clinical assessment, detention, payer, or transport-command decisions that belong to adjacent specialists.
HIPAA or 42 CFR Part 2 limits are ignored and the lane routes protected information without documented authority.
Closure is declared before the receiving program accepts the transfer or before follow-up ownership is assigned.
The industry name is treated as cosmetic and the lane fails to use crisis-continuum artifacts, systems, and vocabulary.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State emergency-detention and involuntary transport rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Jurisdiction-specific detention, emergency petition, and transport rules vary materially and must be supplied by tenant retrieval or human review rather than inferred from the federal baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant implementation of 42 CFR Part 2 and consent workflows
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Programs differ on how Part 2 records are segmented, flagged, and retrieved; the pack requires explicit tenant record-location and disclosure-rule inputs before acting.
